mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during structural labor market shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized strength in consumer demand and construction activity, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive forward returns when RSAFS and HOUST diverge, or the interaction term yields a negative coefficient in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation measured by CPIAUCSL forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy stances, which compresses equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPIAUCSL acceleration, or the signal produces negative Rank IC during periods where inflation is driven by supply shocks rather than demand.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained contraction in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and reduced capacity utilization, which directly suppresses corporate profit margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 60% of cases following three consecutive months of INDPRO declines, or the signal's predictive accuracy vanishes during technology-driven productivity booms.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate robust labor market expansion, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.01 over a 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll revisions consistently coincide with negative short-term equity returns due to inflation fears.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with rising personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based economic demand, which boosts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when GDP growth is driven by inventory accumulation rather than consumption, or the interaction effect reverses sign during financial stress periods.

mechanism_narrative: Deeply negative real interest rates created by low nominal rates and stable inflation stimulate risk appetite and lower the cost of capital, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline despite negative real rates, or the signal loses predictive power when real rate compression stems from deflationary shocks rather than policy easing.
